body.visualDesc: Wide cinematic shot under the Big Oak at dusk. Three small chicks frozen in a ring around the golden straw nest with the still pale-cream egg. Pip (cream-white, straw hat and red bandana) has her wing hovering mid-air above the shell, eyes wide, breath held. Mo (blue-grey, red knitted scarf, tall red comb) sits perfectly still, wings folded, watching. Bo (tan, MUST wear her round thin wire-framed eyeglasses and green scarf) has both wings pressed to her own cheeks in suspense. CRITICAL: the eggshell is CLOSED with exactly TWO thin hairline cracks, a faint warm glow through the cracks only - NOT split, NOT open. Golden-amber light pours through the oak leaves. Fireflies begin to glow. The world holds its breath.
